label: 24_obamacare_aca_repeal_care act
description: Efforts by Republicans to repeal the Affordable Care Act gained momentum as they took control of Congress and the White House. Concerns from American citizens during town halls slowed the process, while political tensions escalated into what some called a 'battle' over healthcare reform.

earliest_articles.title: The Scouting Report Web Chat: Health Care Reform Legislation
earliest_articles.excerpt: Reforming the American health care system was a key aspect of President Obama’s agenda for 2009. Now that bills have passed both the House and the Senate, health care reform may be within reach—although significant hurdles remain. Not all experts are happy with the legislation. Some argue reform will be too expensive.…

earliest_articles.title: Around the Halls: Scott Brown’s Special Election Victory and the Congressional Agenda
earliest_articles.excerpt: With Scott Brown’s victory in the Massachusetts special election for the U.S. Senate, the 60-seat Democratic super-majority is about to disappear. Brown has promised to be the 41st vote against the current health care reform bill, calling the passage of the legislation into question. Scholars from around the halls of…

earliest_articles.title: The Status Report: Obama and Health Care Reform
earliest_articles.excerpt: One year ago, Brookings experts wrote a series of 12 memos to the incoming president on the most pressing policy issues facing the country. Now they assess the administration’s progress on those issues in The Status Report, a daily series of commentary with video to be featured in POLITICO’s Arena. Because Congress…

newest_articles.title: Obamacare’s popularity is the Republicans’ problem
newest_articles.excerpt: Speaking to the National Association of Counties in March of 2010, roughly two weeks before President Barack Obama signed the Affordable Care Act into law, then-House Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) offered a comment for which she would take much grief. “We have to pass the bill,” she said, “so that you can find out…

newest_articles.title: Health care costs are a top concern for voters in the 2024 election
newest_articles.excerpt: Although overshadowed in the national political discourse by discussions about the importance of the economy, immigration, or abortion, voters have expressed that health care continues to be one of their highest priorities. In fact, a May 2024 poll by Pew found that health care was the third-highest issue priority for…

central_articles.title: What Brookings experts are saying about Affordable Care Act (Obamacare) repeal/replace
central_articles.excerpt: Now that Republicans control both Congress and the White House, talk of repealing and replacing the Affordable Care Act (aka “Obamacare”) has accelerated. But as Americans nationwide voice concerns in congressional town halls about repeal, the process may not proceed as rapidly as ACA opponents had hoped. What are the…

central_articles.title: Ordinary politics: Continuation of the health reform war by other means
central_articles.excerpt: The military strategist, Carl von Clausewitz, famously remarked “war is the continuation of politics by other means.” In the battle over the Affordable Care Act (aka ‘Obamacare’), it now seems possible that politics may become the continuation of war by other means. For the past six years, Republicans have been waging…
